mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates tightening capacity utilization and rising corporate revenues, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods where capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 215.0, or the negative correlation vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative 20-day returns in >65% of observed instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 5-year rolling window, or earnings revisions do not follow GDP prints.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal imminent economic contraction and falling corporate cash flows, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The 2-month decline signal yields positive 5-day returns in >70% of historical instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
